findings.summary: Comment blocks repeatedly restate obvious behavior instead of documenting rationale
evidence: src/shared/lib/queryDefaults.ts:47,52,57,65,77,84 add branch comments that mirror the exact condition/return logic immediately below (e.g., abort/auth/client/transient/server/unknown).
src/shared/hooks/shots/index.ts:1,4,12,15,18,28 uses section headers that simply repeat the export group names without additional context.
src/shared/lib/debug/debugRendering.ts:7 and 37 label functions as dev-only, which is already explicit from `if (import.meta.env.DEV)` and function naming.
findings.suggestion: Remove comments that only paraphrase nearby code and keep comments for non-obvious intent (e.g., why loop threshold is 15 per 1000ms, why specific query retry buckets exist, or compatibility constraints).
findings.reasoning: This is a common AI-generated debt pattern: high-volume explanatory text with low information density. It increases maintenance and review noise without improving understanding.
